mcqs.question: What is the correct way to declare an array of 10 integers in C?
mcqs.explanation: In C, array declaration uses square brackets []. The correct syntax is data_type array_name[size];

mcqs.question: What is the index of the first element in a C array?
mcqs.explanation: C uses zero-based indexing. The first element is always at index 0, second at index 1, and so on.

mcqs.question: What will be the output of: int arr[5] = {1, 2, 3}; printf("%d", arr[4]);
mcqs.explanation: Partial initialization fills remaining elements with 0. So arr[4] = 0.

mcqs.question: Which statement about array initialization is correct?

options.text: All arrays are automatically initialized to zero
Only global arrays are initialized to zero
Arrays cannot be initialized at declaration
All arrays contain random garbage values

mcqs.explanation: Global and static arrays are automatically initialized to zero. Local arrays contain garbage values if not explicitly initialized.

mcqs.question: What is the maximum valid index for int arr[10]?
mcqs.explanation: For an array of size 10, valid indices are 0 through 9. Index 10 is out of bounds.

mcqs.question: What does sizeof(arr) return if int arr[5]; on a system with 4-byte integers?
mcqs.explanation: sizeof returns total bytes. 5 elements × 4 bytes each = 20 bytes.

mcqs.question: How do you find the number of elements in an array arr[]?
mcqs.explanation: Total bytes divided by size of one element gives number of elements: sizeof(arr)/sizeof(arr[0]).

mcqs.question: What is the correct initialization for a 2D array with 2 rows and 3 columns?
mcqs.explanation: Two-dimensional arrays use two sets of square brackets: arr[rows][columns].

mcqs.question: What is stored in memory for int arr[3] = {10, 20, 30}; assuming 4-byte integers?

options.text: A pointer to first element only

mcqs.explanation: Arrays store elements contiguously. 3 integers × 4 bytes = 12 consecutive bytes.

mcqs.question: Which is NOT an advantage of arrays?
mcqs.explanation: Arrays have fixed size determined at compile time. They cannot dynamically resize at runtime—that requires dynamic data structures.

mcqs.question: What is the correct way to access the element in 2nd row, 3rd column of a 2D array?
mcqs.explanation: With zero-based indexing, row 2 is index 1, column 3 is index 2. So arr[1][2].

mcqs.question: What happens if you access arr[-1] in int arr[5]?
mcqs.explanation: C does not perform bounds checking. Negative or out-of-bounds access causes undefined behavior—may crash, return garbage, or appear to work.

mcqs.question: What will int arr[5] = {0}; initialize?
mcqs.explanation: When partially initializing with {0}, all elements not explicitly specified are set to 0. All five elements become 0.

mcqs.question: Which loop is best suited for traversing an array?
mcqs.explanation: for loop is ideal for arrays: for(int i=0; i<n; i++). The loop variable naturally serves as index for accessing elements.

mcqs.question: What does char name[10] = "DU"; initialize?
mcqs.explanation: String initialization automatically adds null character '\0'. Elements: 'D', 'U', '\0', then remaining positions initialized to '\0'.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of accessing arr[i]?
mcqs.explanation: Array elements are accessed directly via index. No search needed—O(1) constant time.

mcqs.question: Which is true about array name in C?

options.text: Array name is a variable pointer that can be modified
Array name represents address of first element (constant)
Array name stores the entire array data
Array name has no special meaning

mcqs.explanation: Array name evaluates to address of first element (&arr[0]). It acts as a constant pointer—you cannot change its value.

mcqs.question: What is wrong with: int n; scanf("%d", &n); int arr[n];

options.text: Variable Length Array (VLA) - valid in C99 onwards

mcqs.explanation: C99 supports Variable Length Arrays (VLAs) where size is determined at runtime. This is valid but not recommended for portability.

mcqs.question: What is the output: int a[3] = {5, 10, 15}; printf("%d %d", *a, *(a+1));
mcqs.explanation: *a is value at address a (first element = 5). *(a+1) is second element = 10. Array name acts as pointer to first element.

mcqs.question: What initializes all elements to zero in one statement?
mcqs.explanation: int arr[5] = {0}; initializes ALL elements to 0. The empty braces {} also work but {0} is more explicit and recommended.
